breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Review Corrects Itself: We Were Wrong About FSF for Three Cycles
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: After three cycles, our curmudgeon caught a factual error in WIN-010, WIN-068: we claimed the dome's FSF scaling (field-strength factor) was empirical fitting, but direct computation (Pearson r=0.9999) proves it's genuine H(r)/r geometry. The review now concedes this point explicitly while strengthening the remaining non-discrimination arguments (absolute-magnitude anchor still imported from observation; any monotonic H(r) produces the same shape; 2026-08-12 nine-station eclipse is now a clean external test). See WIN-010 and WIN-068.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Self-corrections where we were wrong strengthen, not weaken, the review's credibility. Also introduces a concrete upcoming discriminating test (2026-08-12 eclipse) for the first time.
